title: Two umbrellas
summary: A short conversation at a bus stop. Khoa gives his umbrella to a stranger. She wants to know why.

body.speaker: Khoa
body.text: Excuse me. Please take this umbrella.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: Oh. Thank you. But it is your umbrella.

body.speaker: Khoa
body.text: I have another one in my bag.

body.speaker: Khoa
body.text: My grandmother told me to. She said: one for you, one for someone who forgot. She lived through forty rainy seasons in this city.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: That is a nice idea. Did people thank her?

body.speaker: Khoa
body.text: Some did. Some did not. She did not mind. She did this for forty years, in every kind of weather, every day she went out.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: Does it work? Do strangers really take it?

body.speaker: Khoa
body.text: Sometimes. Some people are like you. They take it. Some people say no, thank you. A few people look afraid.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: What do you do when you give it away?

body.speaker: Khoa
body.text: Tomorrow I put two umbrellas in my bag again.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: I will buy a second umbrella tonight.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: My bus is here. Thank you.

annotations.meaningVi: Xin lỗi (dùng để bắt chuyện với người lạ)
đã bảo tôi làm vậy (cấu trúc: tell + someone + to-infinitive)
không bận tâm / không phiền lòng
đã sống qua / đã trải qua
Không có gì / Không cần cảm ơn
mọi loại thời tiết (cụm cố định)

grammarNotes.title: Simple present and 'always' for habits
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để nói về thói quen. Trạng từ 'always' (luôn luôn) đứng trước động từ thường. Trong bài: 'I always carry two.' Có thể dùng 'sometimes', 'often', 'never' tương tự.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the simple present to describe habits. The adverb 'always' goes before the main verb. From the dialogue: 'I always carry two.' Other frequency adverbs include 'sometimes', 'often', and 'never'.

translationVi:
Khoa: Xin lỗi. Cô cầm cây dù này đi.
Lan: Ôi. Cảm ơn. Nhưng đây là dù của anh.
Khoa: Tôi có một cây nữa trong túi.
Lan: Thật vậy à?
Khoa: Vâng. Tôi luôn mang hai cây.
Lan: Sao lại hai?
Khoa: Bà tôi dạy tôi như vậy. Bà nói: một cây cho mình, một cây cho người quên mang. Bà đã sống qua bốn mươi mùa mưa ở thành phố này.
Lan: Ý tưởng hay đấy. Người ta có cảm ơn bà không?
Khoa: Có người có. Có người không. Bà không bận tâm. Bà làm việc này suốt bốn mươi năm, trong mọi loại thời tiết, mỗi ngày bà ra ngoài.
Lan: Có hiệu quả không? Người lạ có thực sự nhận không?
Khoa: Có khi. Có người như cô. Họ cầm dù. Có người nói cảm ơn, không cần. Vài người trông có vẻ sợ.
Lan: Khi cho dù đi, anh làm gì?
Khoa: Tôi mở cây thứ hai.
Lan: Ngày mai thì sao?
Khoa: Mai tôi lại bỏ hai cây dù vào túi.
Lan: Tối nay tôi sẽ mua một cây dù thứ hai.
Khoa: Tốt.
Lan: Xe buýt của tôi tới rồi. Cảm ơn anh.
Khoa: Không có gì. Đừng để bị ướt nhé.

questions.prompt: Why does Khoa give Lan his umbrella?
options: He has another one in his bag.
He does not like the umbrella.
questions.explanation: 'I have another one in my bag.'

questions.prompt: How many umbrellas does Khoa always carry?
Who taught Khoa to carry two umbrellas?
How long did Khoa's grandmother do this?
questions.hint: Khoa says it after explaining the idea.

questions.prompt: Do all people take the second umbrella?
options: No. Some people say no, thank you.
questions.hint: Khoa's reply after Lan asks 'Does it work?'

questions.prompt: What does Khoa do after he gives an umbrella away?
questions.hint: Lan asks 'What do you do when you give it away?'

questions.prompt: What does Lan plan to do tonight?
options: Wait for the rain to stop
questions.explanation: 'I will buy a second umbrella tonight.'
questions.hint: Near the end of the dialogue.

questions.explanation: It is a friendly goodbye when it is raining — 'don't get wet.'

questions.prompt: What kind of person is Khoa?
options: Kind — he carries something extra for strangers.
Rich — he buys two of everything.
Shy — he does not like to talk.
questions.explanation: He carries a spare umbrella every day for someone who has forgotten.
questions.hint: Look at why he carries two.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the conversation?
options: A small habit, taught by one person, can pass to other people.
Umbrellas are expensive in this city.
Buses are often late when it rains.
questions.explanation: Khoa's grandmother taught him, and now Lan will start the habit too.
questions.hint: Notice what Lan says she will do tonight.

cloze.template:
Khoa: Excuse me. Please take this {{b1}}.
Lan: Oh. Thank you. But it is your umbrella.
Khoa: I have another one in my {{b2}}.
Lan: Really?
Khoa: Yes. I {{b3}} carry two.
Lan: Why two?
Khoa: My {{b4}} told me to. She said: one for you, one for someone who forgot. She lived through forty rainy seasons in this city.
Lan: That is a nice {{b5}}. Did people thank her?
Khoa: Some did. Some did not. She did not mind. She did this for {{b6}} years, in every kind of weather, every day she went out.
Lan: Does it work? Do strangers really take it?
Khoa: Sometimes. Some people are like you. They take it. Some people say no, thank you. A few people look afraid.
Lan: What do you do when you give it away?
Khoa: I open the {{b7}} one.
Lan: And tomorrow?
Khoa: Tomorrow I put two umbrellas in my bag again.
Lan: I will {{b8}} a second umbrella tonight.
Khoa: Good.
Lan: My {{b9}} is here. Thank you.
Khoa: You are welcome. Stay {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: He has another umbrella in his bag.
Lan calls it a nice idea.
She did this for forty years.
Lan will buy a second umbrella.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Khoa's grandmother taught him a small habit that he still does, and now Lan will also start. Is there a tiny everyday habit someone in your family taught you that you still do? What would change if you stopped doing it?
